name: West: Mixed Vegetables Jaipur Style (Sabji Ja
recipeInstructions:
This makes a delicious and colorful vegetarian entree.
1. Dry-roast cumin and coriander seeds in a small skillet over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Heat oil in the same pan, add onion, and stir-fry until brown. Add the roasted cumin-coriander mixture, fried onions, tomato paste, cashew nuts, and half of the half-and-half. Blend into a smooth paste and set aside.
3. Heat ghee in a heavy 2-quart saucepan over medium-high heat. Add cloves, cardamom pods, and cinnamon stick. Stir until fragrant.
4. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add the remaining vegetables and stir-fry for 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the ground paste, remaining half-and-half, water, cayenne pepper, salt, and raisins. Cover and cook over medium heat until vegetables are tender.
6. Garnish with mint sprigs and serve.
Nutritional Information:
- PER SERVING: 150 calories, 3 g protein, 14 g carbohydrate, 70 g fat (5 g saturated), 21 mg cholesterol, 210 mg sodium, 3 g fiber.
